title: TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE - 211info
text: CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportation for medical appointments, personal business, and shopping. Priority is given to life-sustaining medical appointments. Rides are provided within Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties. The Ride Together program allows riders to recruit their own volunteer driver (i.e., family, neighbors, and friends). Participants schedule rides with their volunteer driver. Mileage reimbursement is provided, and volunteer training is required. The Vets Driving Vets program matches veterans who volunteer their services with other veterans who need rides to doctors' appointments or to take care of other basic needs. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Age 65 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Transportation services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 655-8208 3 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 655-8640 Main phone Message Phone (503) 655-8208 ext 3 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call to request application packet. Approved enrollment must be completed before scheduling rides. FEES: None; donation requested DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas County SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at the intersection of Kaen and Beavercreek Roads, at the top of the hill in Oregon City. On TriMet Bus Line 33 with sign: Clackamas Community College/CCC Services: Disability Related Transportation General Paratransit/Community Ride Programs Non-Emergency Medical Transportation * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Non-Emergency Medical Transportation * Older Adults Non-Emergency Medical Transportation * Veterans Senior Ride Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ADMINISTRATION COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE–MEDICAID INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SENIOR COMPANION PROGRAM INFORMATION SERVICES - FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM AND RELATIVES AS PARENTS REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES UTILITIES - UTILITY SUPPORT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES
